message:
fix(stores): add memory eviction caps to prevent OOM from unbounded growth

All in-memory data stores (history, DMCA, appeals, ratings, reports,
reviews, share-links, support tickets) used Maps that grew without
bound. A bot continuously hitting endpoints without a visitor cookie
could inflate these indefinitely until the process OOM-crashed.

Each store now enforces a hard cap (10k-100k entries depending on
expected volume) and evicts the oldest 10% when exceeded, amortizing
the cleanup cost. The eviction follows insertion order since Maps
preserve it, making the oldest entries the first keys iterated.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
